rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verify capabilities BEFORE asserting.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Plan-mode activation and permission-modes URL within compendium freshness; maintainer block dates citations explicitly (2026-04-22).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Known correct Claude Code facts (Plan mode = Shift+Tab, NOT Shift+P).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body uses 'Shift+Tab until the status bar shows plan' — matches canonical fact in compendium §6.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Sessions are the unit of work.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Exercise has no session widget — the plan-mode shift happens inside the existing M2 session opened upstream by the module file.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No as-is imports — fork + LICENSE + attribution.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Pocock grill-me forked into curriculum/skills/external/pocock-skills/grill-me/ (SKILL.md present on disk); MIT license + commit 62f43a1 + re-verification date 2026-05-15 all named in maintainer block.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks; open-hook for genuine student input.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: push-back-on-the-plan-1 uses open-hook ('My task:') for the student's backlog task — correct per rule 1d. No bracket placeholders in any fence.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: All three included prompts carry semantic lead-ins (plan the task; walk down branches; name the design pattern).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: All prompts render as `**Prompt** *(Claude Code)*` + fenced block via expand-md.js; mode-aware sentinels (⟦M:plan⟧) on plan-mode prompts; no custom labels.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Skills invoke by name, never by path.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No skill invocations inside fenced prompts. Grill-me referenced only as design credit in maintainer block.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Chain by back-reference + deterministic path.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: P2 prompt back-references 'the current plan' (file written by P1); P3 references 'this session' (scrollback). Chain robust.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Preamble before action — design around it (4+ sub-steps / plan-mode trigger).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Body around push-back-on-the-plan-1 frames the long plan-mode wait explicitly ('Plan mode takes minutes on real codebases. Eight to twelve isn't unusual'); paragraph after the fence cues the student on what to look for in the plan file. Preamble absorbed into design.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strong-bold prompt label is exactly **Prompt**.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Expanded output emits `**Prompt** *(Claude Code)*` for each prompt; renderer match preserved.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No module-name references in prompt prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Prompt bodies contain no M-tokens. Body uses 'next module's concern' rather than 'M3'; maintainer-block M-references exempt.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Repeated Q&A needs operator-trigger gating + sectional edits + slowness watch.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: push-back-on-the-plan-2 carries the five-element shape: 'Don't touch the plan file until I say lock it in' (trigger), 'use Edit on the changed section, not Write on the whole file' (sectional), three-at-a-time batching (cadence), the 'If it feels sluggish, ask why' blockquote callout right after the fence (slowness-watch), and the explicit ## Approve section (operational trigger home).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: permission-mode frontmatter + chip when mode matters.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Plan-mode-dependent prompts (push-back-on-the-plan-1, -2, -2-original) carry ⟦M:plan⟧ sentinel in expanded output (frontmatter permission-mode field declared). push-back-on-the-plan-3 (post-approval, default mode per body 'Out of plan mode now') carries no chip — correctly absent.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source URL + type label.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Maintainer block 'Frameworks riffed on' carries URL + [practitioner direct] label on Anthropic permission-modes (2026-04-22), Pocock grill-me (commit 62f43a1, re-verified 2026-05-15), and Klaassen compound engineering. Martin ('What would have to be true') is cultural-vocab — name-only attribution acceptable per §1 carve-out.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Freshness — 6 months.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: All cited dates (2026-04-21 Klaassen, 2026-04-22 Anthropic docs, 2026-05-15 Pocock re-verification) within 6 months of audit date 2026-05-22.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Zombie stat guard.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No round-number AI failure stats cited.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Load-bearing forcing functions ('three at a time', 'Don't touch the plan file until lock it in', 'use Edit on the changed section') live inside push-back-on-the-plan-2 fence; body callouts (slowness-watch, timebox) sit alongside as recovery moves per rule 48.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verification machinery needs smoke-test.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Exercise builds no verification machinery — pure plan-reading + push-back. Judge-building lands in M3.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Context-loading tiers.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Context-loading tier reveal is not the beat of this exercise.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Shift+Tab until the status bar shows plan
Claude Code writes plan file to its plan directory with descriptive name

citations_checked.claim: Pocock grill-me skill, MIT, commit 62f43a1, re-verified 2026-05-15
Roger Martin 'What would have to be true' (Playing to Win)
